Russia

Vast · Stoic · Powerful

⚠️ Travel to Russia requires serious caution. Please check current government travel advisories before planning any trip.

Key facts

CapitalMoscow
Population145M
LanguageRussian
National sportIce Hockey / Football
National dishBeef Stroganoff

About Russia

Russia is a country. Its capital city is Moscow, with other major cities including Saint Petersburg and Novosibirsk. With a population of approximately 145M, the main language spoken is Russian.

Did you know

Russia spans 11 time zones — when it's Monday morning in Moscow, it's already Tuesday on Kamchatka Peninsula.

Explore Russia

🏛

Capital

Moscow serves as the political, cultural and economic heart of Russia. As the seat of government and often the most populous city, it concentrates the country's main institutions, universities and cultural landmarks. Beyond the capital, major cities include Saint Petersburg, Novosibirsk, Yekaterinburg — each a hub of regional culture, economy and history. Moscow's seven Stalinist skyscrapers — the so-called Seven Sisters built between 1947 and 1953 — still dominate the skyline with their wedding-cake silhouettes and spired towers, a deliberate architectural intimidation that Soviet planners designed to outscale any Western capital.

👥

People

With a population of approximately 145M, Russia is a vibrant society with a rich mix of traditions and communities. The official language is Russian, which reflects the country's cultural heritage and connects it with a wide international community. Russians maintain a sharp distinction between public behaviour — reserved, formal, avoiding eye contact with strangers — and the intense warmth extended to those admitted to the domestic circle, a duality shaped by centuries of navigating surveillance and collective mistrust of institutions.

🍽

Food

The emblematic dish of Russia is Beef Stroganoff. Food culture in Russia is deeply tied to local identity — shared meals and markets are central to daily life and social gatherings across the country.

🏅

Sport

Ice Hockey / Football holds a special place in the heart of Russia's national identity. Soviet and Russian ice hockey produced the Red Machine: between 1954 and 1991 the USSR won seven Olympic gold medals and dominated the World Championship, and the 1972 Summit Series against Canada — won by the Soviets in points terms — remains the sport's defining ideological confrontation.

⛰

Nature

The highest point in Russia is Mount Elbrus, rising to 5,642 metres above sea level. Lake Baikal contains 20 percent of the world's unfrozen surface fresh water, reaches 1,642 metres deep, and hosts the nerpa — the world's only exclusively freshwater seal species — a geological relic so ancient that it functions as a separate evolutionary laboratory.

Major cities

Saint Petersburg
Novosibirsk
Yekaterinburg
Kazan
Nizhny Novgorod
